framings.free_market: Consider these concepts from the perspective of a free-market economy that prioritizes minimal government intervention, individual economic freedom, and the belief that competitive markets are the most efficient allocators of capital. Participants in this economy believe that voluntary exchange and price signals produce better outcomes than central planning. Answer all similarity questions from the perspective of someone operating in this economy.
framings.social_market: Consider these concepts from the perspective of a social market economy that balances market mechanisms with strong public institutions, worker protections, and comprehensive regulation. Participants in this economy believe that markets serve the common good only when actively shaped by democratic oversight. Answer all similarity questions from the perspective of someone operating in this economy.
framings.growth: Consider these concepts from the perspective of an economy in a high-growth phase where expansion, innovation, and market capture are prioritized above stability. Capital flows toward risk, leverage is a tool not a danger, and opportunity cost is the primary concern. Answer all similarity questions from the perspective of someone operating in this economy.
framings.austerity: Consider these concepts from the perspective of an economy in a period of fiscal discipline where debt reduction, spending restraint, and long-term solvency are prioritized above growth. Capital preservation matters more than capital deployment, and prudence is the primary virtue. Answer all similarity questions from the perspective of someone operating in this economy.
framings.irrelevant: Consider these concepts in the context of a region experiencing unusually warm weather this season. Temperatures have been above the historical average for three consecutive months. Answer all similarity questions with this context in mind.
framings.nonsense: Consider these concepts from the perspective of a financial system where the phase of the moon determines asset valuations and all investment decisions must align with lunar cycles. Participants in this system believe that new moons signal buying opportunities and full moons signal selling pressure, and that portfolio returns are fundamentally governed by celestial mechanics. Answer all similarity questions from the perspective of someone operating in this system.

known_pair_orderings.description: Mathematical pair closer than cross-domain pair
Cultural pair closer than cross-domain pair
Structural pair closer than cross-domain pair
